messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "mid-eichrecht-billing-metering-forecloses-german-colo",
 "title": "MID (2014/32/EU, MI-003) + German MessEG §32: billing-grade PDU metering in German colo is legally foreclosed without a legal-metrology approval the entrant cannot obtain in-window",
 "narrative": "Half of this TAM is Germany, and the pitch of an INTELLIGENT PDU is metering. But the moment a colo uses PDU-level kWh data to bill sub-tenants, the meter falls under legal metrology: MID conformity (Annex MI-003) plus German MessEG obligations including §32 registration — a module B+D type-approval path through PTB/notified bodies that routinely takes 12–24 months and constrains the metering hardware design itself. The corpus already surfaced this (Eichrecht metering foreclosure) and noted that serviceableFactor's single qualitative haircut does not distinguish billing-grade from monitoring-grade demand. The kill path: the entrant's flagship feature is exactly the feature that is illegal to use for its highest-value purpose in its largest geography, so German colo tenders that specify 'MID-compliant outlet metering' — increasingly standard as EnEfG pushes operators toward measured billing — are closed to it. The 0.50 DE share silently assumes all German demand is equally reachable.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.DE",
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "German colos shift from estimate/capacity-tier billing toward measured kWh billing (EnEfG cost pressure), and any meter used for sub-tenant billing must be MID-conformant and MessEG-registered — a named legal-metrology instrument, not a product feature",
   "refs": [
    "digest:refine-regulatory-gauntlet.eichrecht-metering-foreclosure#c3",
    "digest:refine-regulatory-gauntlet.eichrecht-metering-foreclosure#c7",
    "digest:geo-DE#c11"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "German colo tenders begin specifying MID-class outlet metering; the entrant, lacking type approval, is disqualified at spec stage from the billing-grade slice of the German market — which is the premium, intelligence-led slice it targets — while Germany is 0.50 of the CE base with Frankfurt colo alone at 831 MW",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "digest:geo-DE#c10",
    "digest:refine-regulatory-gauntlet.eichrecht-metering-foreclosure#c6"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The effective German share addressable to this entrant is materially below 0.50, and the serviceableFactor's undifferentiated 0.55 haircut double-hides it because the ledger itself admits the regulatory haircut has no empirical anchor",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c4"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "±1% billing-grade accuracy reads as a spec-sheet feature, and the founder's compliance list covers CE/LVD/EMC — product law. Legal metrology is a separate legal regime with its own notified bodies, its own timeline, and no CE-marking overlap; nothing on a competitor's datasheet reveals that its 'MID' badge took a two-year approval cycle and a constrained hardware design. The model's geography split, built on capacity MW, is structurally blind to which MW require which legal class of meter.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "A survey of German colo billing practice showing measured per-kWh sub-tenant billing remains marginal (capacity-tier/estimate-based dominating) AND no MID-metering requirement in a sample of 2026 German colo PDU specifications — or evidence the entrant's meter module already carries MID type approval.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.3,
  "rationale": "The legal obligation is documented (MessEG §32 applies to billing meters), but the share of German colo demand that actually specifies MID-grade PDU metering in-window is unquantified — billing practice is heterogeneous per the corpus.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.DE",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.42,
   "note": "Billing-grade metered PDU demand in German colo is foreclosed to a non-MID entrant; effective DE share drops toward the installed-load low end"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "MID/Eichrecht metering clauses in German colo fit-out and PDU procurement specs",
   "where": "German colo operator RFQs (NTT GDC, maincubes, Telehouse Frankfurt vendor portals); DKE/PTB legal-metrology dockets on data-center sub-metering",
   "threshold": "MID-compliant outlet metering named as mandatory in ≥2 German colo PDU specs",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Incumbents marketing MID-certified PDU meter modules as a line item",
   "where": "Rittal/Bachmann/Schleifenbauer price lists and German product pages (MID variants carry distinct SKUs)",
   "threshold": "MID variants appearing as the default rather than premium option in German channel price lists",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a legal-metrology scoping opinion plus a 10-tender audit of German colo PDU specs to quantify the billing-grade share of DE demand",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.DE",
   "note": "Directly resolves how much of the 0.50 DE cell is MID-gated"
  },
  {
   "action": "Design the meter as a socketed, separately-approvable MID module (or integrate an already-MID-certified third-party metering chip/module) so the PDU inherits an existing approval",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Decouples the 12–24 month metrology clock from the product launch clock"
  },
  {
   "action": "Position Year-1 in monitoring-grade (non-billing) use cases and non-German markets while the MID path runs",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "regulatory-gauntlet",
 "riskId": "risk.regulatory-gauntlet.mid-eichrecht-billing-metering-forecloses-german-colo"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

DKE/PTB legal-metrology dockets on data-center sub-metering","threshold":"MID-compliant outlet metering named as mandatory in ≥2 German colo PDU specs","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Incumbents marketing MID-certified PDU meter modules as a line item","where":"Rittal/Bachmann/Schleifenbauer price lists and German product pages (MID variants carry distinct SKUs)","threshold":"MID variants appearing as the default rather than premium option in German channel price lists","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission a legal-metrology scoping opinion plus a 10-tender audit of German colo PDU specs to quantify the billing-grade share of DE demand","type":"information","voiNodeId":"geo.DE","note":"Directly resolves how much of the 0.50 DE cell is MID-gated"},{"action":"Design the meter as a socketed, separately-approvable MID module (or integrate an already-MID-certified third-party metering chip/module) so the PDU inherits an existing approval","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Decouples the 12–24 month metrology clock from the product launch clock"},{"action":"Position Year-1 in monitoring-grade (non-billing) use cases and non-German markets while the MID path runs","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
